skillTree.localised_description: The turbulent Winds of Magic can do little harm to those who have found harmony amidst its raging currents.
The best way to repel the chaotic flurry of missile fire is to meet it with order, discipline, and a stalwart row of shields.
Having learned much in their long years of service, this character now turns their attention to training others.

effects.description: Character will share +15% of experience earned between all other active Lords

skillTree.localised_description: Western Cathay’s far-flung deserts gave Bhashiva's ancestral line a place to hone its ways and bring forth the world’s most resilient desert warrior.

effects.description: Replenishes 25% campaign movement range after winning a battle

attributes.bullet_text: Entities with this attribute double their charge bonus when attacking the rear or flank of an entity.

skillTree.localised_description: The White Tiger revels in endlessly stalking her prey, despite it being a long, drawn-out process of studying weakness only bearing fruit at the final pounce.

related_attributes.bullet_text: If this unit can hide at its current location it will not be spotted until the enemy is very close.

skillTree.localised_description: When a hunter cannot help but hate their prey, extra effort must be taken to centre one’s soul and ensure the kill is righteous.

effects.description:

	(Lord's army)

Melee attack: +6 when fighting against Ogre Kingdoms, Warriors of Chaos, Daemons of Chaos, Khorne, Tzeentch, Nurgle and Slaanesh
	(Lord's army)

skillTree.localised_description: Precision beats power. Timing beats speed. Reputation precedes greatness.

related_attributes.bullet_text: This unit frightens all enemy units, reducing their leadership when nearby. It is also immune to fear. Fear penalties do not stack.

effects.description:
Physical resistance: 10% for all Tiger Warrior units
	(Lord's army)

Attribute: Causes Fear for Tiger Warrior units
	(Lord's army)

related_attributes.bullet_text: This unit frightens all enemy units, reducing their leadership when nearby. It is also immune to fear. Fear penalties do not stack.

skillTree.localised_description: As Bhashiva increases her standing, her Tiger Court strengthens its influence under Zhao Ming, consolidating the White Tiger’s military and political prestige.

effects.description: Allegiance points gained: +50% for alliances with Empire, High Elves, Kislev, Bretonnia, Dwarf and Cathay factions

skillTree.localised_description: Those who hunt and scout for Bhashiva’s host must live up to the extremely high standards set by the White Tiger herself.
Hand-to-hand combat is a lethal match of cut and thrust. However, it’s not all about offensive strikes; parries and other defensive moves can also win the day.
Some creatures charge home with such fury that the very ground shakes beneath their feet.
Full-Plate armour protects the wearer from head-to-foot. Many such suits are blessed or imbued with arcane abilities.
The scars on this storied warrior reveal a veteran of many battles, a being not easy to kill and one worthy of respect and fear.
A deadly blade is especially lethal when in the hands of this master death-maker, killer and slaughterer!
From one bloodied, lifeless corpse to the next, this one is always looking for the next adversary to best.
Such is their power and ferocity, a wound-maker will ravage the foe, cutting and pulverising until there is only one exit - death.
This warrior is one with their blade; it is an extension of their will, making them a worthy foe in combat.
The iron will and indomitable presence of this leader ensures their followers remain steadfast when facing peril.
A blade that sings in defence will not let its wielder be penetrated.
The war is endless. The cycle of charging, clashing, and bloody hand-to-hand fighting is without end. It will never stop.
There are Lords that radiate power, confidence and fear, inspiring their followers to great or terrible acts.

effects.description:
Unit experience gain per turn: +75
	(Lord's army)

skillTree.localised_description: The Dragon Emperor guides every sword and spear of the northern provinces, inspiring a strength of arms to match an unflinching strength of will.

skillTree.localised_description: Those who know the land and its secrets will travel faster and further than the foes who don't.
This Lord expects utter obedience from the smallfolk, and has little time for insurrections or petty revolts.
All warriors of honour and pride are eager to fight under the Dragon-Emperor's banners.
The presence of this Lord ensures a measure of calm, reducing panic and desertion amongst followers.

skillTree.localised_description: Through our acts, the gods will cleanse the lands.
Every wound that is not fatal is but a lesson to learn from.

skillTree.localised_description: "The time to strike is now!"

skillTree.localised_description: A good quartermaster can ration equipment and foodstuffs efficiently, making supplies last longer.
Proficient with figures, this Lord, or his subordinates, can keep his army fulfilled, even in dire circumstances.
Countless conflicts and a keen sense of awareness have made this Lord difficult to entrap.

enabled_if: No enemy presence in ability range

attributes.bullet_text:
This unit is permanently visible to the enemy, regardless of line of sight.
	
	Revealed units are not affected by the following attributes:
	 Hide Forest
	 Stalk
	 Unspottable
Entities with this attribute double their charge bonus when attacking the rear or flank of an entity.

factionEffects.localised_description: In the Iron Dragon’s domain, the brightest and best invariably find their way into his service. Bhashiva, leader of the legendary Tiger Warriors and their Court, serves as one of the eight Lords of Shang-Yang.

effects.description:
Lord of Shang-Yang:
	 Can maintain a limited number of Lords and Cathayan units
	 Tiger Court – Improves settlement infrastructure and Tiger Warrior units
	 Armies of Shang-Yang – Allows access to Cathayan units and army improvements
Diplomatic relations: +60 with The Western Provinces

Upkeep: -15% for all Tiger Warrior units
	(all armies)

items.colour_text: This ornate set of katara was crafted for the first White Tiger on the order of the Iron Dragon himself, in gratitude for services to Shang-Yang.
This magical broach was brought to Shang-Yang by traders many centuries ago from an unknown land, its great power destined for warriors of great cause.

effects.description: Replenishes 5% campaign movement range after winning a battle

target_if: Not on or below a platform

ui_effects.localised_text: Bhashiva will teleport to the selected location within range
Bhashiva will strike enemy combatants upon arrival
Causes severe disruption to enemy formations

backgroundSkills.localised_description: The Tiger Warriors believe that when Chaos is ascendant, a White Tiger is born to lead them to salvation – a sacred duty counterbalanced by terrible burden.
